title: My Family: Learning English and Spanish
description: A vocabulary pack for K-2 ELL newcomer students to learn common family members in English and Spanish, featuring a mini-book and three interactive worksheets.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A friendly woman with a warm smile, representing a mother.
A kind man with a gentle smile, representing a father.
A young girl playing, representing a sister.
A young boy playing, representing a brother.
An elderly woman with glasses and a kind expression, representing a grandmother.
An elderly man with a warm smile, representing a grandfather.
A happy baby crawling or sitting.
A diverse group of people (mom, dad, kids) holding hands, smiling.
A friendly woman standing next to a child, representing an aunt.
A friendly man standing next to a child, representing an uncle.
A young boy playing with another child, representing a male cousin.
A young girl playing with another child, representing a female cousin.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the words for family members.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras de los miembros de la familia.

worksheets.instructions_en: Draw a line from the English word to its picture.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Dibuja una línea desde la palabra en inglés hasta su imagen.

items.image_prompt: A friendly woman with a warm smile.
A kind man with a gentle smile.
A friendly woman standing next to a child.
A friendly man standing next to a child.

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the pictures of the family members.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Colorea los dibujos de los miembros de la familia.

items.content: A picture of a family (mom, dad, two children) smiling together.
A picture of a mom hugging a child.
A picture of a dad playing with a child.
A picture of a girl with a bow, representing a sister.
A picture of a boy with a toy car, representing a brother.
A picture of a grandma reading a book.
A picture of a grandpa telling a story.

pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a happy family (mom, dad, two children) smiling together.

pages.text_en: This is my family. / Esta es mi familia.
pages.text_l2: Esta es mi familia. / This is my family.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a diverse family (mom, dad, two children) smiling.

pages.text_en: This is my Mom. / Esta es mi Mamá.
pages.text_l2: Esta es mi Mamá. / This is my Mom.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a kind mom.

pages.text_en: This is my Dad. / Este es mi Papá.
pages.text_l2: Este es mi Papá. / This is my Dad.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a friendly dad.

pages.text_en: This is my Sister. / Esta es mi Hermana.
pages.text_l2: Esta es mi Hermana. / This is my Sister.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a happy sister.

pages.text_en: This is my Brother. / Este es mi Hermano.
pages.text_l2: Este es mi Hermano. / This is my Brother.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a playful brother.

pages.text_en: This is my Grandma. / Esta es mi Abuela.
pages.text_l2: Esta es mi Abuela. / This is my Grandma.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a sweet grandma.

pages.text_en: This is my Grandpa. / Este es mi Abuelo.
pages.text_l2: Este es mi Abuelo. / This is my Grandpa.
pages.image_prompt: A drawing of a kind grandpa.

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and name common family members in English and Spanish, using simple sentences.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and realia. Use the mini-book for shared reading and individual practice. Worksheets can be completed independently or in small groups. Encourage students to talk about their own families.
differentiation_tips: For emerging students: Provide sentence frames ('This is my ____.') and point to pictures. Allow students to only identify words in their stronger language first.
For developing students: Encourage them to use both English and Spanish words. Ask them to describe one family member using an adjective.
For expanding students: Have them create their own sentences about their family members. Ask them to draw and label additional family members not covered in the pack (e.g., pets).
